---
title: "401k_double_ml_extended"
author: "Alexander Unger"
date: "2026-05-22"
output: html_document
---
```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# BLOCK E — WALD-AIPW VIA DoubleML (IIVM)
# ==============================================================================
# Applies the DoubleML vignette approach (DML_smoothers.rmd) to the 401k data.
#
# Design:
#   Instrument Z = e401  (eligibility)
#   Treatment  D = p401  (participation)  — binary
#   Outcome    Y = net_tfa
#   Covariates X = age, db, educ, fsize, hown, inc, male, marr, pira, twoearn
#
# Estimator: DoubleMLIIVM  (Wald-AIPW / LATE)
# Learners:  XGBoost for outcome + propensity models
#            (requires specific hyperparameters for omega'Y = tau_hat to hold)
# ==============================================================================

library(DoubleML)
library(mlr3verse)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Learner setup
# XGBoost hyperparameters required so that the smoother matrix SY = Y_hat holds,
# which is the condition for omega'Y to reproduce the point estimate exactly.
# (See vignette: alpha=0, subsample=1, max_delta_step=0, base_score=0)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
regr_xgb  <- lrn("regr.xgboost",
                  alpha         = 0,
                  subsample     = 1,
                  max_delta_step = 0,
                  base_score    = 0)
classif_xgb <- lrn("classif.xgboost",
                    alpha         = 0,
                    subsample     = 1,
                    max_delta_step = 0,
                    base_score    = 0.5)

# IIVM needs three learners:
#   ml_g : outcome model  E[Y | X, Z=z]   — regression
#   ml_m : treatment model P(D=1 | X, Z)  — classification
#   ml_r : instrument model P(Z=1 | X)    — classification
ml_g_iivm <- regr_xgb$clone()
ml_m_iivm <- classif_xgb$clone()
ml_r_iivm <- classif_xgb$clone()

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Build DoubleML data object from pension data
# DoubleMLData$new() needs a data.frame with named columns
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
pension_df <- data.frame(
  net_tfa = Y,
  p401    = D,
  e401    = Z,
  age     = pension$age,
  db      = pension$db,
  educ    = pension$educ,
  fsize   = pension$fsize,
  hown    = pension$hown,
  inc     = pension$inc,
  male    = pension$male,
  marr    = pension$marr,
  pira    = pension$pira,
  twoearn = pension$twoearn
)

iivm_data_401 <- DoubleMLData$new(
  pension_df,
  y_col  = "net_tfa",
  d_cols = "p401",
  z_cols = "e401",
  x_cols = c("age", "db", "educ", "fsize", "hown",
             "inc", "male", "marr", "pira", "twoearn")
)

cat("DoubleML data object:\n")
print(iivm_data_401)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fit DoubleMLIIVM
# store_models = TRUE and store_predictions = TRUE are required for
# get_outcome_weights() to work
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
set.seed(1234)
cf_folds <- 5

iivm_obj_401 <- DoubleMLIIVM$new(
  iivm_data_401,
  ml_g = ml_g_iivm,
  ml_m = ml_m_iivm,
  ml_r = ml_r_iivm,
  n_folds = cf_folds
)

cat(sprintf("\nFitting Wald-AIPW (IIVM) with %d-fold cross-fitting...\n", cf_folds))
iivm_obj_401$fit(store_models = TRUE, store_predictions = TRUE)

cat("\nDoubleML Wald-AIPW result:\n")
print(iivm_obj_401$summary())

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Extract outcome weights
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\nExtracting outcome weights...\n")
omega_waipw_401 <- get_outcome_weights(
  object   = iivm_obj_401,
  dml_data = iivm_data_401
)

# Algebraic identity check: omega'Y should reproduce the point estimate
identity_check <- all.equal(
  as.numeric(omega_waipw_401$omega %*% Y),
  as.numeric(iivm_obj_401$all_coef)
)
cat("omega'Y replicates point estimate?", identity_check, "\n")



```

```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# BLOCK F — WEIGHT DIAGNOSTICS FOR WALD-AIPW
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n")
cat("==============================================================================\n")
cat("WEIGHT DIAGNOSTICS — Wald-AIPW (DoubleMLIIVM), 401(k)\n")
cat("==============================================================================\n")

w_waipw <- as.vector(omega_waipw_401$omega)

diag_tab <- weight_diag(w_waipw, "Wald-AIPW (IIVM)")
print(diag_tab, row.names = FALSE)

cat(sprintf("\nSum of weights:       %10.6f  (should be ~0 for translation invariance)\n",
            sum(w_waipw)))
cat(sprintf("Effective sample size: %9.1f  (out of N = %d)\n",
            1 / sum(w_waipw^2), length(w_waipw)))
cat(sprintf("Negative weights:      %9.1f%%\n",
            mean(w_waipw < 0) * 100))
cat(sprintf("Max |weight|:          %9.6f\n",
            max(abs(w_waipw))))

# Compare against kappa weights from Block earlier
# (requires kw_ml from kappa_outcome_weights — recompute if needed)
p_ml_401 <- logit_mle(Z, X_401_kappa)
p_cb_401 <- get_cbps_p(Z, X_401_kappa)
kw_ml    <- kappa_outcome_weights(Z, D, p_ml_401)
kw_cb    <- kappa_outcome_weights(Z, D, p_cb_401)

cat("\n--- Comparison: Wald-AIPW vs kappa estimators ---\n")
comp_tab <- rbind(
  weight_diag(w_waipw,        "Wald-AIPW (DML)"),
  weight_diag(kw_cb$w_u,      "tau_cb_u"),
  weight_diag(kw_ml$w_u,      "tau_ml_u"),
  weight_diag(kw_ml$w_a10,    "tau_ml_a10"),
  weight_diag(kw_ml$w_a,      "tau_ml_a"),
  weight_diag(kw_ml$w_a1,     "tau_ml_t"),
  weight_diag(kw_ml$w_a0,     "tau_ml_a0")
)
print(comp_tab, row.names = FALSE)

```


```{r}
#===============================================================================
# BLOCK G — LOVE PLOTS FOR WALD-AIPW
# ==============================================================================
# Covariate balance check using DML outcome weights.
# Flip sign for untreated: multiply by (2*D - 1) for cobalt compatibility.
# We compare Wald-AIPW weights against the raw (unadjusted) differences and
# also against tau_ml_u (best normalized kappa) for direct visual comparison.
# ==============================================================================

cat("\nGenerating love plots...\n")

# Flip weights for cobalt
w_waipw_flipped  <- w_waipw * (2 * D - 1)
w_u_ml_flipped   <- kw_ml$w_u * (2 * D - 1)
w_u_cb_flipped   <- kw_cb$w_u * (2 * D - 1)
w_a10_flipped    <- kw_ml$w_a10 * (2 * D - 1)

# Love plot 1: Wald-AIPW alone
love_waipw_only <- love.plot(
  D ~ X_dml_401,
  weights    = list("Wald-AIPW (DML)" = w_waipw_flipped),
  position   = "bottom",
  title      = "Wald-AIPW — 401(k)",
  thresholds = c(m = 0.1),
  var.order  = "unadjusted",
  binary     = "std",
  abs        = TRUE,
  line       = TRUE,
  colors     = viridis(2),
  shapes     = c("circle", "triangle")
)

# Love plot 2: Wald-AIPW vs best normalized kappa (tau_ml_u) — direct comparison
love_waipw_vs_kappa <- love.plot(
  D ~ X_dml_401,
  weights = list(
    "Wald-AIPW (DML)" = w_waipw_flipped,
    "tau_ml_u (kappa)" = w_u_ml_flipped,
    "tau_ml_a10 (kappa)" = w_a10_flipped
  ),
  position   = "bottom",
  title      = "Wald-AIPW vs Kappa Estimators — 401(k)",
  thresholds = c(m = 0.1),
  var.order  = "unadjusted",
  binary     = "std",
  abs        = TRUE,
  line       = TRUE,
  colors     = viridis(4),
  shapes     = c("circle", "triangle", "diamond", "square")
)

love_waipw_only
love_waipw_vs_kappa

# Combined figure
figure_waipw_401 <- grid.arrange(
  love_waipw_only,
  love_waipw_vs_kappa,
  nrow = 1
)

cat("\nBLOCK G complete.\n\n")



```
